target.name: Palantir
target.founder: Peter Thiel, Alex Karp, Stephen Cohen, Joe Lonsdale, Nathan Gettings

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Palantir is a public-stage AI & Software company currently valued above $400 billion following its transition from private markets, where it raised over $3 billion. The comparable universe for valuation includes one public peer (PLTR itself) and eight private-stage AI & Software companies, including Waymo, Figure AI, Applied Intuition, Skild AI, and Crusoe Energy.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable in this case. The AI & Software sector contains twelve same-sector peers overall, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints in each category—below the minimum threshold required for meaningful peer analysis. Applying an all-sector median would introduce significant distortion given Palantir's unique position as a publicly traded, large-cap software company operating in enterprise data platforms and AI-driven analytics.

The scarcity of direct comparables at Palantir's scale and maturity limits triangulation to market pricing alone. As a public company, its valuation is set by continuous market transactions rather than derived from comparable multiples or other analytical methods. Key uncertainties center on the sustainability of current public market valuations given the breadth of its addressable markets and execution risk across government and commercial segments. Traditional valuation frameworks prove insufficient for frontier-stage AI companies operating at this capitalization scale.
